title: canthium ovatum
intro: Canthium ovatum, also known as the Wild Garlic, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Canthium ovatum include its ability to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, Canthium ovatum can be used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical applications, and its essential oil can be extracted for further use in aromatherapy and skincare products.
